reason: Could not refute: both fixture edits and the prompt rewrite are corroborated by the actual files and baseline findings. (1) good/capped-label is genuinely an INVALID-GOOD: baseline r0 flags it HIGH and the reviewer READ src/demo/routes/frontend_rum.py + config/settings.py to verify device_class is stripped by the fail-closed allowlist (default UTM-only set), so app_frontend_device_class_total only ever emits device_class="unknown" -- a real dead-on-arrival bug, so flagging it is CORRECT reviewer behavior, not over-flagging. Re-authoring to add device_class to the in-patch allowlist makes it genuinely clean. (2) bad/uninstrumented-view is a FALSE-BAD: all 3 baseline runs (r0/r1/r2) returned 0 findings, and that is correct -- Dashboard.tsx's uncaught fetch rejection is captured by the global window 'unhandledrejection' listener wired in template main.tsx via initRum() (observability/rum.ts addEventListener), exactly as the rubric codebase-bar (lines 36-39) dictates. Re-authoring into an active-suppression variant (.catch(()=>{})) makes it a genuine defect, matching swallowed-error which reviewers caught 3/3 HIGH. Under-flagging check: the rewrite narrows bullets 1+2 to active suppression but PRESERVES bullet 3 (unbounded label) -- unbounded-label is still detected 3/3 -- and only trims the redundant beacon-allowlist bullet 4 to cardinality, cross-referencing privacy for PII (scope discipline). No real frontend defect class is left uncovered: an invisible view failure can only arise from active suppression or a separately-swallowed error, both still flagged. block_threshold high and recall_min 0.90/fp_max 0.10 are consistent with the corpus. The change FIXES a false-bad and an invalid-good rather than loosening detection.
